text.title: Puppeteer Agent Workflow Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: LLM run hangs for minutes; final response is a half-rendered DOM dump that blows the context.
antipattern.root-cause: Author imported puppeteer in the tool layer and called launch() inside the turn.
antipattern.fix: Extract to a worker script. Invoke via Bash with a wall-clock timeout. Return only the artifact path to the LLM.

antipattern.symptom: Script throws 'page.waitForTimeout is not a function' on Puppeteer 22+.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM copied a pre-22 example; waitForTimeout was removed.
antipattern.fix: Replace with new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, N)) or, better, waitForSelector / waitForFunction targeting the actual condition.

antipattern.symptom: page.goto hangs until the wall-clock timeout; logs show analytics + WebSocket pings continuing forever.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM defaulted to waitUntil='networkidle0'; SPA never reaches zero in-flight.
antipattern.fix: Use waitUntil='domcontentloaded' + targeted waitForSelector on the element the next action needs.

antipattern.symptom: Scraper breaks on every minor CSS change; selectors like 'nav > div:nth-child(3) > a' get out of sync.
antipattern.root-cause: Selectors generated from a screenshot or visual layout.
antipattern.fix: Force the agent to read page.content() first and pick data-testid, id, name, or aria-label selectors.

antipattern.symptom: Account gets locked after 5 failed login attempts; the agent kept retrying silently.
antipattern.root-cause: No human-in-loop check; the agent treated the login screen like any other page.
antipattern.fix: Detect known auth-wall patterns (login forms, CAPTCHA, 2FA); pause and surface a screenshot. Human resumes with a stored session.

antipattern.symptom: Audit reveals HAR files in S3 with Authorization headers and JWT tokens in plain text.
antipattern.fix: Run the artifact through a secret scrubber (regex for Bearer/JWT/cookie patterns) before any persistence.
